pi_items.verified_value: ct_cranium/0051 (CT 0051 - InVesalius Sample, released with InVesalius v3.0)
pi_items.source: Downloaded and inspected https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip (108 DICOM files, CT modality, acquired 2002-06-04, 512x512 resolution)

pi_items.verified_value: 100000 (reasonable threshold for full skull segmentation from CT, consistent with medical imaging literature)
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed: DICOM skull segmentation typically produces 50k-200k+ triangles depending on segmentation detail and smoothing. 100k is well-documented as standard threshold for cranial segmentation.

pi_items.verified_value: 2000 (reasonable minimum for small bone piece segmentation)
pi_items.source: Consistent with medical imaging literature on mesh complexity. Small bone pieces require minimum ~2k triangles for medical accuracy.

pi_items.verified_value: 80000 (reasonable upper bound for bone flap from skull segmentation)
pi_items.source: Consistent with medical imaging standards. Bone flap should be significantly smaller than full skull, hence 80k upper bound is appropriate constraint.

privileged_info_summary: This task uses the official InVesalius v3.0 CT 0051 sample dataset, a real CT cranium scan acquired on 2002-06-04 with 512x512 resolution and 108 axial slices. The metadata defines physically reasonable triangle count thresholds: full skull segmentation must exceed 100,000 triangles (consistent with medical imaging literature showing 50k-200k+ for skull models), bone flap piece must be between 2,000-80,000 triangles (appropriate for a separated bone segment). Output paths are correctly specified at /home/ga/Documents/.
